problem_addressed: Conventional RGB-D solutions for humanoid manipulation suffer from narrow fields of view and self-occlusion, requiring frequent base movements that introduce motion uncertainty and safety risks in large workspace scenarios.
core_claim: OmniDP enables robust humanoid manipulation in large workspaces through omnidirectional 3D perception using an end-to-end LiDAR-driven 3D visuomotor policy.
key_contributions: Development of OmniDP, an end-to-end LiDAR-driven 3D visuomotor policy for large-workspace manipulation
Time-Aware Attention Pooling mechanism for processing sparse and temporally flickering 3D point cloud data
Whole-body teleoperation system for efficient data collection on full-body coordination
novelty: Unlike prior work that primarily used RGB-D cameras or third-view point clouds, this paper introduces panoramic LiDAR-based perception for humanoid manipulation, addressing the fundamental limitation of narrow fields of view that constrain effective workspace.
method_summary: The method processes panoramic LiDAR point clouds through a Time-Aware Attention Pooling mechanism, encoding historical sequences of 3D data while capturing temporal dependencies, and uses a Diffusion Policy decoder to generate coordinated whole-body actions.
method_components: Perception encoder with pyramid convolutional architecture enhanced with time-aware attention
Diffusion Policy decoder for action generation
Time-Aware Attention Pooling (TAP) mechanism for handling sparse 3D data
Whole-body teleoperation system using XR controllers for data collection
key_innovation: Time-Aware Attention Pooling mechanism that aggregates point clouds over a short temporal window and uses attention weights conditioned on temporal offsets to create a temporally smoothed representation, addressing the instability of standard global max pooling over single-frame sparse LiDAR observations.
assumptions: LiDAR provides sufficient geometric information for manipulation tasks
Objects beyond 1.3 meters from the sensor provide limited utility for manipulation
Upper-body joints can be controlled independently of pre-trained lower body controllers
theoretical_grounding: The paper relies on empirical validation through extensive experiments in simulation and real-world environments rather than formal mathematical proofs or theoretical bounds.
datasets_used: Teleoperated trajectories using TeleVuer framework (size not specified)
hyperparameters: 20 trials per task, point clouds downsampled to 4096 points for baselines, LiDAR point clouds with distance-based cropping and uniform downsampling
headline_result: OmniDP achieved 82/120 total success rate across all tasks (Table I)
ablations: Removing omnidirectional LiDAR input causes complete failure (0/20 success)
Disabling time-aware attention pooling reduces success rate from 12/20 to 9/20
improvement_over_baseline: OmniDP outperforms baselines by 2-13x in total success rate and achieves 14/20 success in collision-free tasks where all baselines achieve 0%
stated_limitations: Conventional RGB-D solutions suffer from narrow fields of view (FOV) and self-occlusion
Existing approaches to expanding perception introduce mechanical complexity, calibration dependencies, and latency
Physical constraints prevent the robot from repositioning itself in some scenarios
unstated_weaknesses: No quantitative comparison with traditional RGB-D approaches in the abstract
Limited information about computational requirements and hardware specifications
Unclear how well the approach scales to even larger workspaces
No discussion of failure cases or specific limitations in particular scenarios
threats_to_validity: Evaluation might be limited to specific environments or object types
Potential overfitting to the training scenarios
Possible evaluation bias if the authors designed the test scenarios
open_questions: How does the approach perform with different types of sensors or sensor configurations?
What are the computational requirements for real-time operation?
How does the approach handle dynamic environments with moving objects?
Can the methodology be adapted for other robotic platforms?
What are the failure modes and how can they be mitigated?
future_work: Extending to more complex manipulation tasks
Evaluating in more diverse and unstructured environments
gaps_this_enables: New approaches for handling self-occlusion in robotic vision
relevance_to_topic: This paper is tangentially relevant to the project as it explores omnidirectional perception and active learning concepts, but its focus on robotics rather than medical imaging limits direct applicability.
best_result: OmniDP achieved 82/120 total success rate across all tasks (Table I)
limitations_stated: Conventional RGB-D solutions suffer from narrow fields of view (FOV) and self-occlusion
Existing approaches to expanding perception introduce mechanical complexity, calibration dependencies, and latency
Physical constraints prevent the robot from repositioning itself in some scenarios
title: OmniDP: Beyond-FOV Large-Workspace Humanoid Manipulation with Omnidirectional 3D Perception
